2603.15926.title: Evaluating Causal Discovery Algorithms for Path-Specific Fairness and Utility in Healthcare
2603.15926.paper_summary: Causal discovery in health data faces evaluation challenges when ground truth is unknown. We address this by collaborating with experts to construct proxy ground-truth graphs, establishing benchmarks for synthetic Alzheimer's disease and heart failure clinical records data. We evaluate the Peter-Clark, Greedy Equivalence Search, and Fast Causal Inference algorithms on structural recovery and path-specific fairness decomposition, going beyond composite fairness scores. On synthetic data, Peter-Clark achieved the best structural recovery. On heart failure data, Fast Causal Inference achieved the highest utility. For path-specific effects, ejection fraction contributed 3.37 percentage points to the indirect effect in the ground truth. These differences drove variations in the fairness-utility ratio across algorithms. Our results highlight the need for graph-aware fairness evaluation and fine-grained path-specific analysis when deploying causal discovery in clinical applications.
2603.15926.paper_summary_zh: 因果發現於健康數據面臨評估挑戰，當真實情況未知時。我們通過與專家合作構建代理真實圖來解決這個問題，為合成阿茲海默症和心臟衰竭臨床記錄數據建立基準。我們評估了 Peter-Clark、Greedy Equivalence Search 和 Fast Causal Inference 算法在結構恢復和路徑特定公平性分解上的表現，超越了綜合公平性分數。在合成數據上，Peter-Clark 實現了最佳的結構恢復。在心臟衰竭數據上，Fast Causal Inference 實現了最高的效用。對於路徑特定效應，射出分數對於真實情況中的間接效應貢獻了 3.37 個百分點。這些差異驅動了不同算法之間公平性-效用比的變化。我們的結果突顯了在臨床應用中進行因果發現時，需要進行圖感知的公平性評估和細緻的路徑特定分析。
2603.15926.author: Nitish Nagesh et.al.
2603.15926.authors: Nitish Nagesh, Elahe Khatibi, Thomas Hughes, Mahdi Bagheri, Pratik Gajane, Amir M. Rahmani
